mission.title: caddy: Improve performance of FastCGI transport (and clean up code)
mission.description: Improve performance of FastCGI transport (and clean up code). This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy analyze symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Improve performance of FastCGI transport (and clean up code)

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant caddy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
The current `fastcgi` implementation is functional, but clunky and slow.

Go does not have a standard FastCGI client implementation (note that we are not a _responder_ we are a client), so in January

steps.title: Apply the fix for Improve performance of FastCGI transport (and clean up code)
steps.description:
Trying to fix [3803](https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy/issues/3803).

I tried it on a small php site, no problem so far. Extensive testing is required.
```yaml
RUN xcaddy build --with github.com/WeidiDeng/caddy/tree/fastcgi-improve
```

steps.title: Confirm Improve performance of FastCGI transport (and clea is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Trying to fix [3803](https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy/issues/3803).

I tried it on a small php site, no problem so far. Extensive testing is required.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
